assertions.title: RecoveryPhraseScreen — renders the mnemonic and confirms
assertions.reason: The focused recovery-phrase Jest suite passed, proving the screen renders every mnemonic word exactly once in order for both 24-word and 12-word cases, exposes the exact ‘I’ve saved it’ anchor, and fires onConfirm exactly once.

commands: rg -n -e '^\s*it\(' -e 'renders the full 24-word mnemonic in order exactly once each' -e 'renders the confirm button with matching text and accessibilityLabel' -e 'invokes onConfirm exactly once when the confirm button is pressed' -e 'supports a 12-word mnemonic \(render-order invariant applies\)' src/features/auth/screens/recovery-phrase-screen.test.tsx
testNames: renders the full 24-word mnemonic in order exactly once each
renders the confirm button with matching text and accessibilityLabel
invokes onConfirm exactly once when the confirm button is pressed

assertions.title: RecoveryPhraseScreen — mnemonic is never persisted
assertions.reason: The lifecycle persistence test passed after mount, confirm, and unmount, with zero secure-storage or NativeBiometricVault writes containing mnemonic data and an explicit assertion that setSecureItem is never called.

commands: rg -n -e 'never writes the mnemonic \(or any individual word\) to any storage across the lifecycle' src/features/auth/screens/recovery-phrase-screen.test.tsx
testNames: never writes the mnemonic (or any individual word) to any storage across the lifecycle

assertions.title: RecoveryPhraseScreen — optional Copy affordance is bounded
assertions.reason: This implementation takes the contract’s absence branch: the Jest suite explicitly asserts there is no Copy affordance, so clipboard copy never auto-fires and any future silent addition will fail the test.

commands: rg -n -e 'does NOT render a Copy affordance \(clipboard TTL is vacuously satisfied\)' src/features/auth/screens/recovery-phrase-screen.test.tsx
testNames: does NOT render a Copy affordance (clipboard TTL is vacuously satisfied)

assertions.title: FLAG_SECURE on Android RecoveryPhraseScreen and RecoveryRestoreScreen
assertions.reason: Android FLAG_SECURE coverage passed at both levels: the RecoveryPhraseScreen unit test observed enable on mount and disable on unmount, and the native shim integration test proved the calls forward to NativeModules.EnboxFlagSecure.activate/deactivate under the canonical module name.

testNames: enables Android FLAG_SECURE on mount and clears it on unmount
canonical JS name matches the Kotlin FlagSecureModule.NAME contract

assertions.title: iOS app-switcher obscuring for recovery screens
assertions.reason: The iOS AppState test passed: inactive and background transitions add the opaque privacy cover, active removes it again, and a companion Android assertion confirms the cover is not redundantly rendered there because FLAG_SECURE handles that platform.

commands: rg -n -e 'renders an opaque cover view on iOS when AppState becomes inactive or background' -e 'does NOT render the iOS cover view on Android' src/features/auth/screens/recovery-phrase-screen.test.tsx
testNames: renders an opaque cover view on iOS when AppState becomes inactive or background
does NOT render the iOS cover view on Android (FLAG_SECURE handles app-switcher)

assertions.title: RecoveryPhraseScreen disables back-navigation after confirmation
assertions.reason: The navigation-options test passed and the component implementation explicitly sets gestureEnabled:false, headerBackVisible:false, and headerLeft:null, matching the contract’s back-navigation lockout requirements for the mnemonic screen.

assertions.title: Clipboard TTL when Copy affordance exists
assertions.reason: The screen exposes no Copy affordance, so the contract marks clipboard-TTL enforcement as vacuously satisfied; the explicit absence test passed and guards against a later unbounded clipboard feature landing silently.

commands: rg -n -e 'does NOT render a Copy affordance \(clipboard TTL is vacuously satisfied\)' src/features/auth/screens/recovery-phrase-screen.test.tsx
testNames: does NOT render a Copy affordance (clipboard TTL is vacuously satisfied)

assertions.title: Rapid-tap debounce on biometric CTAs
assertions.reason: The contract-targeted onboarding-edge-cases Jest subset passed: triple synchronous presses on the biometric setup CTA yielded exactly one initializeFirstLaunch call, triple presses on the biometric unlock CTA yielded exactly one unlockAgent call, and a post-resolution press re-armed correctly instead of being permanently disabled.

commands: rg -n -e 'VAL-UX-047' -e 'debounces BiometricSetup CTA' -e 'debounces BiometricUnlock CTA' -e 're-arms after a resolved attempt' src/features/auth/screens/__tests__/onboarding-edge-cases.test.tsx
testNames: debounces BiometricSetup CTA: 3 synchronous presses yield exactly one initializeFirstLaunch call
debounces BiometricUnlock CTA: 3 synchronous presses yield exactly one unlockAgent call
re-arms after a resolved attempt — a fresh press after completion re-enters the action

commandsRun.observation: PASS; the focused recovery-phrase suite passed all 11 tests.
PASS; the native flag-secure integration suite passed all 6 tests.
PASS; the contract-targeted debounce subset ran the 3 VAL-UX-047 tests successfully.

commandsRun.observation: PASS; the flag-secure native test scan captured the canonical EnboxFlagSecure name and the mount/unmount bridge assertions.

commandsRun.observation: PASS; the VAL-UX-047 test-file scan located the three debounce assertions by line.

summary: Tested 8 assigned assertions on the jest-local surface: 8 passed, 0 failed, 0 blocked. RecoveryPhraseScreen, native FLAG_SECURE integration, and contract-targeted biometric CTA debounce coverage all passed, and the saved evidence logs map each assertion to explicit test names and source lines.
